verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is canonically a secreted serine protease with no TM domain, abundantly released into prostatic fluid and circulation. However, a 2025 CCR study (Shen et al.) systematically confirmed cell-surface expression of KLK2 on prostate cancer cell lines (VCaP) and patient-derived bone-metastatic tissue by FACS and immunofluorescence co-localized with EpCAM. This surface pool is prostate cancer/AR-signaling-restricted. Clinical-stage programs including a CD3×KLK2 bispecific T-cell engager (pasritamig, NCT04898634), CAR-T, and 225Ac-RLT all engage surface KLK2 on tumor cells. Surface display is real but restricted to prostate cancer (AR+) context, qualifying as contextual/cell_state_induced (oncogenic-state ecto-display in a tissue-restricted lineage).
